sections.description:
MBR Advanced Asset Manager helps you optimise your WordPress site by selectively blocking unnecessary CSS and JavaScript files on specific pages, cutting page weight by 1–2MB on average. Everything runs locally — no external services, API keys, or data sent to third parties.

Key features

  • Local scanning — scans pages, posts and public custom post types using WordPress's built-in HTTP loopback, no external APIs.
  • Device-specific rules — block assets for mobile, desktop, or both.
  • Block on all pages — toggle any asset to be blocked globally across the whole site.
  • All post types — manage assets on pages, posts, WooCommerce products, or any public CPT.
  • Preview (dry run) — test blocking rules before saving them.
  • Size analysis — see file sizes to spot the biggest wins.
  • Editor override — temporarily disable blocking for logged-in editors.
  • Page-builder aware — blocking is skipped in Elementor, Bricks, Divi, Beaver Builder, Oxygen and Visual Composer editors.
sections.changelog:
2.5.4

  • Security: Asset URLs, handles and sources parsed from scanned front-end HTML are now escaped before being inserted into the admin scan table, preventing DOM-based XSS in wp-admin.
  • Security: File-size HEAD requests no longer follow redirects, closing an SSRF vector where a public host could redirect the loopback to an internal/reserved address (e.g. 169.254.169.254 or 10.x).
  • Added: Self-hosted automatic update delivery via Plugin Update Checker.

2.5.3

  • Fixed: Rescanning a page no longer hides assets already on the saved blocklist (regression from 2.5.1's anonymous loopback).
  • Added: Token-based scan recognition — each scan uses a one-time token validated against a 60-second transient, replacing the old auth-state check and closing a blocking-bypass hole.
  • Important: If you use the optional asm-blocker.php MU-plugin, re-copy the new file over the old one (MU-plugins do not auto-update). Internal MU version: v6.4.0.

2.5.2

  • Fixed: Saved blocklist now reliably blocks assets for real visitors, not just in preview — the client-side blocker now runs for the full blocklist as a safety net.
  • Changed: Client-side blocker hooks into wp_head priority 2 so it lands before styles/scripts print.
  • Note: Clear all page caches after upgrading.

2.5.1

  • Fixed: Scanner now lists only public front-end assets by fetching pages anonymously, instead of pulling in admin-bar/dashicons assets real visitors never load.
  • Note: Private, draft and password-protected pages can no longer be scanned (they have no public front-end assets).

2.5.0

  • Added: Support for Posts and all public Custom Post Types, grouped by type in the dropdown.
  • Fixed: Button handlers no longer trigger a page refresh after save.
  • Styled: Dark-theme readability improvements.

2.4.0

  • Added: "Block on All Pages" global blocking, global rules counter, and new AJAX endpoints.
  • UI overhaul: Catppuccin Mocha dark interface with toggle switches and colour-coded controls.
sections.installation:
Standard installation

  1. Download the plugin ZIP file.
  2. Go to WordPress admin → Plugins → Add New → Upload Plugin.
  3. Upload the ZIP and activate the plugin.
  4. Go to Settings → Advanced Asset Manager to start optimising.

Optional MU-plugin for enhanced blocking

For the strongest possible blocking (recommended for sites with aggressive lazy-loading or dynamic asset injection):

  1. Copy asm-blocker.php into /wp-content/mu-plugins/ (create the folder if it doesn't exist).
  2. This enables both server-side and client-side blocking for maximum effectiveness.

The MU-plugin is a manual copy and does not auto-update with the plugin — re-copy it after updates that change it.
